title.en: How koreans say "Let's eat soon" — Is it a real invitation?
title.ko: 조만간 밥 한번 먹자! (한국식 인사치레와 다음 기약하기)

description.en: Don't get fooled! Learn the secret behind the famous Korean farewell "Let's grab a meal sometime" and how to respond naturally.
description.ko: "언제 밥 한번 먹자" 라는 말, 진짜 약속일까요? 한국인들의 정겨운 인사치레에 당황하지 않고 센스 있게 대답하는 리액션을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Social. Greeting. Culture. Farewell. Relationship. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina. I’ve been waiting for a phone call for a week! A Korean friend told me. "밥 한번 먹자" which means "Let's have a meal."

content.speaker: John
content.text: But I haven't heard anything from him. Is he ghosting me, or did I miss something?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그건 한국 사람들의 아주 전형적인 '인사치레' 예요.
content.translation: That is a very typical '인사치레' of Korean people.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 헤어질 때 아쉬움을 담아서..., "다음에 꼭 다시 보자" 라는 뜻으로 하는 말이죠.
content.translation: It's something people say when parting to mean "Let's definitely see each other again."

content.speaker: John
content.text: Ah, so it's more like a warm "See you later" than a concrete plan?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 정확해요! 구체적인 날짜를 잡지 않는다면 그냥 기분 좋은 인사로 받아들이면 돼요.
content.translation: Exactly! If they don't set a specific date, you can just take it as a pleasant greeting.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘은 이런 인사치레에 센스 있게 화답하는 법을 배워볼게요!
content.translation: Today, we'll learn how to respond to these greetings with style!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 조만간 밥 한번 먹어요. So I can say this too when I want to be friendly?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네! "당신과 계속 친하게 지내고 싶다" 라는 뜻을 전달하는 아주 좋은 리액션이에요.
content.translation: Yes! It's a very good reaction to convey the meaning "I want to continue to be close to you."

insight.title: 조만간 밥 한번 먹어요
insight.usage_tip: '조만간(Sometime soon)' + '밥(Meal)' + '한번(Once)' + '먹어요(Eat)'. This is the most common Korean farewell phrase that shows friendliness.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 네. 좋아요! 조만간 밥 한번 먹어요.
content.translation: Yes, great! Let's have a meal sometime soon.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What if I want to make sure it's a real plan?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐 "언제가 좋으세요?" 라고 구체적으로 물어보세요.
content.translation: In that case, ask specifically, "언제가 좋으세요?".

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 날짜를 정하기 시작하면 그때부터는 진짜 약속이 되는 거예요!
content.translation: Once you start picking a date, it becomes a real appointment from then on!

insight.title: 언제가 좋으세요?
insight.usage_tip: '언제(When)' + '가(Subject marker)' + '좋으세요(Is good/Polite)'. Use this to turn a vague greeting into a real plan.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. I just ran into an old colleague on the street.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 어? 존! 여기서 다 만나네요. 잘 지냈어요?
content.translation: Oh, Mr. John! What a surprise to see you here. Have you been well?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 미나! 정말 반가워요. 연락하려고 했는데 제가 좀 바빴네요.
content.translation: Yes, Mina! It's so good to see you. I was going to call you, but I've been a bit busy.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 저도요! 우리 오늘 아쉽지만 이만 가봐야겠네요. 조만간 밥 한번 먹어요!
content.translation: Me too! It's a shame, but I guess we have to go now. Let's have a meal sometime soon!

insight.title: 조만간 밥 한번 먹어요
insight.usage_tip: Mina is using this to end the conversation warmly since she's in a hurry.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 네. 좋아요! 제가 조만간 연락드릴게요.
content.translation: Yes, great! I'll contact you sometime soon.

insight.title: 제가 조만간 연락드릴게요
insight.usage_tip: Even if you don't call right away, this is a perfect way to match the other person's friendly vibe.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그래요. 꼭 연락해요! 안녕!
content.translation: Okay, make sure to call! Bye!

content.speaker: John
content.text: I see. It’s not a lie; it’s just a way of showing affection and keeping the door open!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 한국어 리액션의 핵심은 단어의 뜻보다 그 속에 담긴 '따뜻한 마음' 을 읽는 거예요.
content.translation: Right. The core of Korean reactions is reading the 'warm heart' inside rather than just the meaning of the words.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "future promise" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 조만간 밥 한번 먹어요!
content.translation: Let's have a meal sometime!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 제가 연락드릴게요!
content.translation: I'll contact you!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 언제가 좋으세요?
content.translation: When would be good for you?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 조만간 꼭 봐요!
content.translation: Let's see each other soon for sure!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 조심히 가세요!
content.translation: Go safely! / Get home safely!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 헤어지는 친구에게 따뜻하게 말해 보세요. "조만간 밥 한번 먹자!"
content.translation: Everyone, try saying it warmly to a friend you're parting with today. "Let's grab a meal sometime!"

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. We've finished Part 1 of Season 2!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 우리 다음 파트에서 더 재미있는 상황으로 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: Let's meet with more fun situations in the next part. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: "밥 한번 먹자"는 말은 한국인들의 대표적인 인사치레예요. ("Let's eat sometime" is a representative social courtesy of Koreans.)
조만간 좋은 소식을 들려드릴 수 있을 것 같아요. (I think I'll be able to tell you some good news sometime soon.)
그의 대답은 전형적인 한국식 거절 표현이었어요. (His answer was a typical Korean expression of refusal.)
좀 더 구체적인 계획을 세우는 게 좋겠어요. (It would be better to make a more specific plan.)
그녀의 친절에 밝은 미소로 화답했어요. (I responded to her kindness with a bright smile.)
친구들과 정겹게 인사를 나누며 헤어졌어요. (I parted with my friends while exchanging warm greetings.)
